lesson_groups.narrative_spine: One story on the fixed two-hundred-email queue: name why holdout accuracy fails Product at the zero point nine threshold, trace Priya's zero point nine four on Q-041 to an honest per-email claim, then walk score-to-bin-to-count-to-observed-rate so the zero point nine four versus seventy-one percent gap is mechanism—not vibes.
section_outline: Scene: quarantine queue, 148 spam / 52 ham, Product wants 0.9 auto-quarantine, Legal wants proof
Why ninety-one point five percent accuracy is the wrong memo object for routing
Where Q-041's score comes from and what honest would mean on that row
Building the mechanism: sort scores into bins, count outcomes, compare claimed to observed
The high-bin gap (0.94 claimed vs 71% observed) as audit proof the model is lying to Product
omit_from_group: Repeated Scene cards after the opening
Folder spam rate or θ vocabulary
Beta arithmetic or posterior update language
Side tangent on TF-IDF beyond one Terms tonight line

lesson_groups.narrative_spine: Same 200 rows, different memo: Marcus tags one more spam row overnight—prior from 148/52 ledger, likelihood from the single label event, posterior 149/51 signed tonight without retraining TF-IDF weights; frozen routing scores stay on a parallel audit track.
section_outline: Story so far: calibration bins answered, folder rate is the new unknown
Which ledger column moves first when Marcus tags Q-088 spam
Prior, likelihood, posterior as folder-belief language on counts
Beta(148,52) + 1 spam → Beta(149,51) count update without retrain
Memo template Marcus signs; what stays frozen on the routing column
omit_from_group: Re-deriving the high-score bin table from lesson one
Conjugacy proof or full credible-interval arithmetic

lesson_groups.narrative_spine: Stretch bridge on the same 200 emails: Leadership collapses three dashboard numbers—route Product's 0.9 gate to bin audit, Marcus's folder memo to Beta ledger, dev accuracy to internal ranking only; name the clause before opening the spreadsheet.
section_outline: Scene: VP asks why folder rate and Q-041 score aren't interchangeable
Three-row audit table under inv-calibration pressure
Decision fork: Marcus clause vs Product clause on Q-041 deploy
Deploy recommendation and graph checkpoint (E-109, E-110, E-calibration-hub)
Stretch close: pick the audit clause, then pick the tool
omit_from_group: Full reliability-diagram re-derivation from lesson one
Beta posterior arithmetic replay from lesson two

omit_from_narrative: Repeated full Scene-card cold opens inside merged groups—one Scene per published lesson
Wilson-style intervals on sparse quarantine bins
Platt scaling or isotonic regression remap mechanics
MCMC or full credible-interval derivation tonight
Accuracy-as-routing-metric re-litigation after lesson one settles it

review_summary.rationale: All three published lessons honor the approved lesson_groups. Lesson 1 reads as one merged calibration arc (slots 1–3): wrong memo object → per-email score claim → six-step bin audit ending on the 0.94 vs 71% gap, with folder-rate preview deferred cleanly. Lesson 2 stands alone on slot 4 folder-belief update, connects via Story so far without re-deriving bins, and keeps routing scores frozen. Lesson 3 remains a separate optional stretch on slot 5—tool routing under inv-calibration, ~1.5k words, graph checkpoint intact. Editor polish tightened lesson 1 (six-step recipe, stakeholder table, duplicate section removed) without splitting the merged spine or absorbing stretch into core.
